codeReview.summary: Schema infrastructure implementation matches the feature contract: table-name validation, idempotent physical DDL, history triggers, composite history index, system table initialization on DB init, and Database::create_table wiring are all present and covered by tests. No blocking correctness or safety defects were found in the reviewed commit.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-worker skill procedure lists `cargo check --workspace` as part of required full verification, but this session did not run it while still reporting followedProcedure=true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/rust-worker/SKILL.md (Work Procedure step 7) requires cargo check; worker transcript skeleton + handoff verification for session 793b2050-d403-4d1e-b561-16e71600a72e lists cargo test/clippy only.

sharedStateObservations.observation: At this feature commit, architecture docs still described `_cairn_tx_context` as a TEMP table while implementation had switched to a regular system table due SQLite trigger constraints, creating temporary source-of-truth drift.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md lines 21, 35, 89-95 (temp-table wording) vs cairndb-core/src/schema.rs lines 141-170 in commit 854b7d4 (regular `_cairn_tx_context` table and rationale).

summary: Pass: feature implementation is functionally complete and contract-aligned for schema infrastructure, with no blocking code defects identified. Noted shared-state gaps around verification procedure enforcement and temporary architecture-document drift.
